methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $27M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 2 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Astro Mechanica is a Series A-stage company in the Supersonic & Hypersonic sector, having raised $27M+. The comparable universe for this sector is sparse: two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and two private peers (Boom Supersonic, Hermeus) were identified, though this narrow peer set presents meaningful constraints on valuation methodology.

Only one triangulation method proved applicable. The Stage-Progression approach, using total raised of $27M as a proxy baseline and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yields $54M. A Peer-Median approach was not viable due to insufficient same-stage or comparable-tier datapoints within the Supersonic & Hypersonic sector; applying a broad cross-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $46M to $62M, with a median point of $54M. This range reflects the inherent uncertainty in a single-method valuation: stage-progression mechanics are directionally sound but rely on standardized multipliers that may not capture sector-specific dynamics or Astro Mechanica's particular technical or commercial position. Key risks to this range include the early-stage, capital-intensive nature of hypersonic development, limited public market comparables, and the nascent commercial timeline for the sector. A second method—whether refined peer analysis or revenue/development-stage multiples—would be necessary to narrow this range with confidence.
